title: 10x Multiome Gene Expression Library Construction and Sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b0u34","text":"This protocol describes the construction, quality control, and sequencing of 10x Multiome Gene Expression (GEX) libraries. It details library quantification via Agilent Bioanalyzer, multiplexed sequencing on the Illumina NovaSeq X Plus, and initial data processing using Cell Ranger ARC to ensure a target depth of 20,000 read pairs per nucleus.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Construct libraries in accordance with the 10x Genomics Chromium Next GEM Single Cell Multiome ATAC + Gene Expression User Guide (CG000338). CG000338_ChromiumNextGEM_Multiome_ATAC_GEX_User_Guide_RevG.pdf 6 cycles of cDNA amplification are performed for an expected target recovery of 10,000 nuclei. cDNA Quality Control (QC): Assess cDNA quality and size distribution using the Agilent Bioanalyzer High Sensitivity DNA assay. cDNA Quantification: Determine cDNA concentration by selecting the region between 200–9000 bp . Carry forward 25% total cDNA yield to Gene Expression (GEX) Library Construction, and tailor the number of sample index PCR cycles to the cDNA input, according to the User Guide. GEX Library Quality Control (QC): Assess library quality and size distribution using the Agilent Bioanalyzer High Sensitivity DNA assay. GEX Library Quantification: To determine library molarity, calculate the average fragment size by selecting the region between 175–1000 bp . GEX Library Pooling: Prepare an equimolar pool of libraries for multiplexed sequencing and determine pool concentration using the Qubit dsDNA High Sensitivity (HS) assay. Typical Bioanalyzer cDNA trace. Typical Bioanalyzer Gene Expression Library trace.

Target Depth: Sequence GEX libraries to a minimum depth of 20,000 read pairs per nucleus . Sample Sheet Generation: Populate the Illumina SampleSheet.csv using the specific index sequences from the Dual Index Kit TT Set A . Dual_Index_Kit_TT_Set_A.csv NovaSeq X Plus Configuration: Use the following cycling parameters: Read 1: 28 cycles i7 Index: 10 cycles i5 Index: 10 cycles Read 2: 90 cycles

Primary Analysis: Process raw FASTQ files from paired GEX and ATAC libraries using the Cell Ranger ARC pipeline to perform demultiplexing and initial cell calling. Depth Normalization: Evaluate the actual GEX reads per nucleus for each sample. If the target of 20,000 GEX reads is not met, rebalance the GEX library pool and perform supplemental sequencing as required. Downstream Analysis: Proceed to integrated multiomic analysis once target depth and cell recovery metrics are validated.
creator: Scott Lindsay-Hewett
